speaker: SALISBURY
text: I did not think the King so stored with friends.

speaker: PEMBROKE
text: Up once again. Put spirit in the French.

speaker: PEMBROKE
text: If they miscarry, we miscarry too.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: That misbegotten devil, Faulconbridge,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: In spite of spite, alone upholds the day.

speaker: PEMBROKE
text: They say King John, sore sick, hath left the field.

speaker: MELUN
text: Lead me to the revolts of England here.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: When we were happy, we had other names.

speaker: PEMBROKE
text: It is the Count Melun.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Wounded to death.

speaker: MELUN
text: Fly, noble English; you are bought and sold.

speaker: MELUN
text: Unthread the rude eye of rebellion

speaker: MELUN
text: And welcome home again discarded faith.

speaker: MELUN
text: Seek out King John and fall before his feet,

speaker: MELUN
text: For if the French be lords of this loud day,

speaker: MELUN
text: He means to recompense the pains you take

speaker: MELUN
text: By cutting off your heads. Thus hath he sworn,

speaker: MELUN
text: And I with him, and many more with me,

speaker: MELUN
text: Upon the altar at Saint Edmundsbury,

speaker: MELUN
text: Even on that altar where we swore to you

speaker: MELUN
text: Dear amity and everlasting love.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: May this be possible? May this be true?

speaker: MELUN
text: Have I not hideous death within my view,

speaker: MELUN
text: Retaining but a quantity of life,

speaker: MELUN
text: Which bleeds away even as a form of wax

speaker: MELUN
text: Resolveth from his figure ’gainst the fire?

speaker: MELUN
text: What in the world should make me now deceive,

speaker: MELUN
text: Since I must lose the use of all deceit?

speaker: MELUN
text: Why should I then be false, since it is true

speaker: MELUN
text: That I must die here and live hence by truth?

speaker: MELUN
text: I say again, if Louis do win the day,

speaker: MELUN
text: He is forsworn if e’er those eyes of yours

speaker: MELUN
text: Behold another daybreak in the East.

speaker: MELUN
text: But even this night, whose black contagious breath

speaker: MELUN
text: Already smokes about the burning crest

speaker: MELUN
text: Of the old, feeble, and day-wearied sun,

speaker: MELUN
text: Even this ill night your breathing shall expire,

speaker: MELUN
text: Paying the fine of rated treachery

speaker: MELUN
text: Even with a treacherous fine of all your lives,

speaker: MELUN
text: If Louis by your assistance win the day.

speaker: MELUN
text: Commend me to one Hubert with your king;

speaker: MELUN
text: The love of him, and this respect besides,

speaker: MELUN
text: For that my grandsire was an Englishman,

speaker: MELUN
text: Awakes my conscience to confess all this.

speaker: MELUN
text: In lieu whereof, I pray you bear me hence

speaker: MELUN
text: From forth the noise and rumor of the field,

speaker: MELUN
text: Where I may think the remnant of my thoughts

speaker: MELUN
text: In peace, and part this body and my soul

speaker: MELUN
text: With contemplation and devout desires.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: We do believe thee, and beshrew my soul

speaker: SALISBURY
text: But I do love the favor and the form

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Of this most fair occasion, by the which

speaker: SALISBURY
text: We will untread the steps of damnèd flight,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: And like a bated and retirèd flood,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Leaving our rankness and irregular course,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Stoop low within those bounds we have o’erlooked

speaker: SALISBURY
text: And calmly run on in obedience

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Even to our ocean, to our great King John.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: My arm shall give thee help to bear thee hence,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: For I do see the cruel pangs of death

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Right in thine eye.—Away, my friends! New flight,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: And happy newness, that intends old right.
